items.title: Big Caring unveils automated Klang distribution centre and HQ
items.summary: KLANG: Big Caring Group has launched a new distribution centre in Klang, alongside a centralised corporate headquarters, to strengthen its supply chain through technology innovation. The facility handles over 90,000 order lines daily, reflecting growing scale across its operations. Big Caring executive director Lim Sin Yin said the automated distribution centre was built from Covid-19 lessons, where demand volatility and supply chain strain exposed the limits of traditional models and efficiency alone was insufficient.

items.title: China hopes 'relevant parties' can grasp chance at peace in Iran war
items.summary: BEIJING: China's foreign ministry said it hoped the "relevant parties can grasp this chance at peace and bring the region back to stability as soon as possible" after the US and Iran agreed to a ceasefire in the weeks-long Middle East war. China said it has maintained communication with all parties and "actively sought to promote reconciliation and prevent further fighting," foreign ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said in a daily press briefing. Beijing hoped that relevant parties would seize the opportunity for peace, "resolve differences through dialogue and consultation and work towards to early restoration of peace and stability in the Gulf and the Middle East region," Mao said.

items.title: Data centre boom may strain renewable financing, says CIMB Islamic
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The rapid expansion of data centres could constrain financing for renewable energy projects unless Malaysia updates its long-term energy transition assumptions, CIMB Islamic Bank Bhd sustainable finance head Arshad Nuval Othman said. "Data centres have changed the energy demand profile. "This could restrict financing for large-scale energy projects.

items.title: MTT Shipping retail IPO oversubscribed 2.7 times ahead of listing
items.summary: The offering drew 10,863 applications seeking 184.79 million shares worth RM190.33 million, against 50 million shares allocated to the Malaysian public. The Bumiputera public portion received 3,893 applications for 54.14 million shares, or 1.17 times oversubscribed, while the other public portion was oversubscribed by 4.23 times after receiving 6,970 applications for 130.65 million shares. The IPO comprises 633.5 million shares priced at RM1.03 each, including an institutional offering of 571 million shares to Malaysian and foreign investors, as well as Bumiputera investors approved by Miti, and a retail offering of 62.5 million shares to directors, employees, contributors and the Malaysian public.

items.title: Kelantan police chief: Officers fired in self-defence during UiTM Machang machete attack, attempted murder probe under way
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 9 — A man with multiple criminal records was injured after being shot when he attempted to attack police officers with a machete in front of Universiti Teknologi MARA (UiTM) Machang last night. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said police had earlier received information about a shooting at the location at 11.17pm. He said the officers were forced to fire several shots at the suspect in self-defence and to prevent serious injury.

items.title: Ministers: Govt backs Malaysia’s film industry with insolvency support, offers free mediation for disputes under RM250,000
items.summary: Also speaking at the same event, Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said free mediation services will be offered for contract disputes valued below RM250,000. The initiative, she said, reflects the reality that most contracts, particularly within the creative industry, fall within that threshold. This, Azalina said, enables parties to resolve disputes without bearing high legal fees.

items.title: Zahid: Cloud-seeding operation in Perak a success
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The cloud-seeding operations (OPAs) implemented in Perak yesterday have shown positive development, with rainfall recorded in several key water catchment areas in the state, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. He said that a significant amount of rainfall - between 29.3 millimetres (mm) and 84.8mm - was recorded in the Bukit Merah Dam and Temenggor Dam areas. He said the operations in the south would focus on the Machap Dam, Upper Layang Dam, Lower Layang Dam, Lebam Dam and Pulai Dam in Johor and the Durian Tunggal Dam and Jus Dam in Melaka.

items.title: Mercedes reports drop in Q1 sales during 'transition year' for China market
items.summary: Quarterly sales fell six per cent to 419,400 vehicles from the same quarter a year ago, it said in a statement on Thursday. While sales volumes grew by seven per cent in Europe and 20 per cent in the United States, this was not enough to offset a 27 per cent plunge in China. Mercedes and its rival BMW face a cut-throat price war with local brands in China, the world's biggest auto market.

items.title: Asian equities slip on doubts over durability of Mideast ceasefire
items.summary: HONG KONG: Emerging Asian shares retreated on Thursday as new fault lines between the US and Iran raised concerns over the durability of a fragile Middle East ceasefire, exacerbating fears about the potential impact on economies exposed to oil shocks. The MSCI gauge of EM Asia equities slipped about one per cent from a one-month high scaled the prior day, dragged by a 1.6 per cent drop in South Korea's KOSPI. An index tracking global EM stocks eased 0.9 per cent from a five-week high as oil futures rose with energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz staying restricted as Iran flexed control over the vital oil artery.

items.title: ACE Market hopeful Inspace Creation in underwriting deal with TA Securities
items.summary: In a statement today, the interior fitting-out services provider said TA Securities will underwrite 26.97 million new shares under the exercise. The IPO involves the issuance of 68.50 million new ordinary shares, or 18.55 per cent of the enlarged issued share capital of 369.30 million ordinary shares, and an offer for sale of 29.30 million existing shares, or 7.93 per cent. Of the total new shares, 18.47 million will be made available to the Malaysian public, while 8.50 million will be allocated to eligible directors, employees and other contributors.

items.title: Penang scores above 89 per cent in Urban Sustainability Index
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang has recorded an overall score of between 89.13 and 90.32 per cent in the 2025 Urban Sustainability Index (USI) released by the Housing and Local Government Ministry. USI is a framework used to evaluate how cities perform across environmental, social and economic dimensions, especially in addressing rapid urban growth and the challenges posed by climate change Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said both local authorities — Penang Island City Council (MBPP) and Seberang Prai City Council (MBSP) — attained sustainable status based on assessments across key dimensions. "In terms of overall performance, 42 indicators recorded sustainable scores above 0.80, six were moderately sustainable (0.50 to 0.79), while three fell below 0.49, placing them in the less sustainable category," he said after the meeting.

items.title: #NSTviral: Factory bus rams vehicles on 2nd Penang Bridge
items.summary: BATU KAWAN: A video has gone viral showing a factory bus ramming into several vehicles on the Sultan Abdul Halim Mu'adzam Shah Bridge. It is understood that the video was recorded by a car following the factory bus. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Blogger Papagomo faces legal action over false claims against MCMC
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) will take legal action against Wan Muhammad Azri Wan Deris, known as "Papagomo", over alleged false claims involving the commission. In a statement today, MCMC denied that it had blocked a Malaysia Gazette report on his court case involving Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. The commission said it takes the matter seriously and will pursue legal action against Wan Azri over the claims.

items.title: Malaysia, Brunei to empower disaster management commitment via Nadma-NDMC cooperation, says DPM Zahid
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 9 — Malaysia and Brunei will continue to strengthen bilateral cooperation in disaster management, particularly expertise sharing, capacity building and joint training, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. Ahmad Zahid who is also the Central Disaster Management Committee (JPBP) chairman, said the effort was implemented through collaboration between the National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma) and Brunei’s National Disaster Management Centre (NDMC), which has been the main platform for coordinating various initiatives since 2022. He said Malaysia and Brunei also agreed to emphasise the importance of implementing integrated cross-border disaster management exercises regularly as the two countries share a long land border.

items.title: Israel military claims it has killed nephew of Hizbollah chief Naim Qassem
items.summary: JERUSALEM: Israel's military said Thursday it had struck and killed a close adviser to Hizbollah chief Naim Qassem in Beirut a day earlier, when Israeli strikes pummelled Lebanon. "Yesterday, the IDF struck in the Beirut area and eliminated Ali Yusuf Harshi, the personal secretary and nephew of Hizbollah Secretary-General Naim Qassem," a military statement said. Harshi was "a close associate and personal adviser to... Qassem and played a central role in managing and securing his office", it added.

items.title: Spain condemns Israeli attacks on Lebanon, reopens Teheran embassy
items.summary: MADRID: Spain's Foreign Minister Jose Manuel Albares on Thursday accused Israel of violating international law and a newly brokered two-week ceasefire in the Middle East by carrying out airstrikes on Lebanon on Wednesday. "Yesterday we saw how Israel, flouting the ceasefire and in violation of international law, dropped hundreds of bombs on Lebanon," Albares told lawmakers in the lower house. Earlier on Thursday, Albares announced that Spain would reopen its embassy in Teheran in hopes of achieving peace in the region.

items.title: 4 suspects surrender in vicious Triang attack, 2 still at large
items.summary: BERA: Four men wanted over a brutal slashing in Triang last month have surrendered to police. The suspects, aged 27 to 37, including three whose images were previously released, turned themselves in at Bera district police headquarters between April 6 and yesterday. Bera police chief Superintendent Zulkiflee Nazir said that, to date, five suspects have been arrested, while police are tracking two others.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh: ‘Bangun KL’ initiative aims to reduce peak-hour traffic through earlier commutes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 9 — The “ Bangun KL: Ke Arah Kuala Lumpur Yang Lebih Sejahtera ” initiative aims to encourage people to enter the capital earlier, thereby easing peak-hour congestion, said Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories), Hannah Yeoh. She said the initiative focuses on behavioural change through incentives and cross-sector collaboration to address traffic challenges more holistically. That is why we introduced Bangun KL .

items.title: Woman charged again with 44 counts of CBT involving more than RM1.3mil
items.summary: MUAR: A former accounts executive of a wet tissue supply company was charged again at the Sessions Court here today, this time with 44 counts of criminal breach of trust (CBT) involving more than RM1.3 million in company funds. The accused, Goh Hui Lan, 48, pleaded not guilty to all charges before Sessions Court judge Mohd Khairi Haron. According to the charges, the accused allegedly deceived a 34-year-old female company manager into approving payments totalling RM1,352,954.28, which were purportedly meant for suppliers.

items.title: Malaysia’s rare-earth crown: Lynas plans heavy-element takeover to rival China
items.summary: GEBENG, April 9 — Australian mining firm Lynas told AFP on Wednesday it plans to expand its portfolio of rare earths extracted in Malaysia, as it hopes to bolster its position as a key alternative supplier to China. “The first step was to demonstrate that we are able to extract heavy rares (earths),” Lynas chief operating officer Pol Le Roux said in an interview at the company’s facility in eastern Malaysia’s Gebeng. Lynas Malaysia chief operating officer Pol Le Roux (centre) and Lynas Malaysia chief executive officer Amanda Lacaze (right) speak at the Australian mining firm’s Malaysian rare earths refinery in Gebeng, Pahang on April 8, 2026.

items.title: Suspect in Machang police attack remanded for seven days
items.summary: KOTA BARU: A suspect arrested for allegedly attempting to kill a policeman in Machang last night has been remanded for seven days starting today. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said police obtained the remand order when the suspect was produced at the magistrate's court here today. He said the suspect, who has nine prior criminal records, is being investigated under Section 307 of the Penal Code for attempted murder.

items.title: Fisheries Dept: Stockpiles can sustain Malaysia’s fish supply for three months if needed
items.summary: IPOH, April 9 — The country’s fish supply remains unaffected by the global energy crisis stemming from the West Asia conflict, the Department of Fisheries (DOF) director-general Datuk Adnan Hussain said. He said fishing vessel operations in zones A, B and C are continuing normally, supported by government fuel subsidies. Adnan said aquaculture could step in to keep fish supplies steady if sea fishing faces disruptions, even as feed costs are set to rise due to logistics snags, as farmers’ existing stocks will maintain stable output.

items.title: Temporary road closure on Jalan Yew for bridge reconstruction works
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The route along the Jalan Yew bridge will be temporarily closed to all vehicles from 10pm to 5am from April 10 to 21. The closure involves the stretch between TRX and Bulatan Pudu, as well as parts of Jalan Cochrane and Jalan Pasar (from the Masjid Alam Shah junction to the Petron station on Jalan Pasar). "During this period, road users are advised to use alternative routes via Jalan Pudu, Jalan Chan Sow Lin and Bulatan Kampung Pandan to reduce congestion and ensure smoother traffic flow.

items.title: MCMC denies blocking report, to take legal action against blogger Papagomo over false claims
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 9 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) will initiate legal action against blogger Wan Muhammad Azri Wan Deris, better known as Papagomo, over false claims that the commission blocked a news report involving Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. The news report in question, published by the Malaysia Gazette portal, pertained to a court case involving the minister and Wan Muhammad Azri. “In this regard, MCMC will take appropriate legal action against Wan Azri concerning the dissemination of these false claims,” the statement read.

items.title: Energy, water tariff reforms needed as subsidy burden rises, says Fadillah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Subsidies for energy and water are increasingly straining long-term fiscal sustainability and system viability, prompting greater urgency to reform tariff structures, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. Fadillah, who is also the Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister, said subsidies may provide short-term relief but risk creating structural burdens that could hinder investment. "Subsidies may soften the present, but burden the future," he said at the Malaysia Energy, Water and Climate Change Summit 2026 today, adding that a sustainable system must reflect real costs.

items.title: Johor KPDN busts diesel syndicate using modified lorries, seizes 3,300 litres worth RM73,296
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 9 — The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) Johor branch busted a diesel misappropriation syndicate, seizing 3,300 litres worth RM73,296 in an operation at the Sri Purnama Industrial Area today. She said inspections found that the modified lorry was fitted with three Intermediate Bulk Container (IBC) tanks containing liquid suspected to be diesel, amounting to 3,000 litres. “In addition, a tanker lorry was also found to contain liquid suspected to be diesel, estimated at 300 litres, bringing the total seizure to approximately 3,300 litres,” she said in a statement today.

items.title: Govt to test incentive-driven scheme to ease KL morning congestion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government is launching a behavioural-based initiative to tackle Kuala Lumpur's persistent morning congestion, encouraging commuters to enter the city earlier through targeted incentives. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said that the programme, named Bangun Kuala Lumpur! (Bangun KL!

items.title: Man found dead in Mercedes died of heart attack
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 66-year-old man was found dead in his car in front of a restaurant in Batu Caves on Tuesday. Gombak police chief Assistant Commissioner Noor Ariffin Mohamad Nasir said the man died of a heart attack. Earlier, photos and video clips went viral on social media showing the man unconscious in the driver's seat of a Mercedes-Benz.

items.title: Fifth suspect held in RM12,000 Kelantan police bribery probe
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has arrested a man and obtained a five-day remand over a RM12,000 bribery case involving four policemen in Kelantan. "The latest arrest raises the total number of people held in connection with the case to five," the source said. MACC today obtained a five-day remand order against the suspect at the Magistrate's Court.

items.title: Bomba rescues government surveyor injured by fallen tree in Sarawak forest
items.summary: LAWAS, April 9 — A government surveyor was injured after being struck by a fallen tree in the forest at Kampung Murud Suang here today. Lawas Fire and Rescue Station (Bomba) chief Clarence D. Primus Tiandun said the 37-year-old victim from Kampung Berawan, Limbang was found semi-conscious under the fallen tree at around 12pm. “The team, together with Health Ministry personnel, provided initial treatment to the victim at the scene.

items.title: Israeli attacks on Lebanon violate US-Iran ceasefire, says Iranian minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Israeli attacks on Lebanon yesterday constitute a "grave violation" of the United States-Iran ceasefire, Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Saeed Khatibzadeh told the BBC. In an interview on the BBC's Today programme, Khatibzadeh said Iran had sent a message to the Oval Office following the strikes, warning Washington against taking contradictory positions. Khatibzadeh said the United States "must choose" between war and peace.

items.title: Zara Qairina's case: Trial of teacher over spreading false information resumes in June
items.summary: Magistrate Suriani Ujang set the dates for the trial involving Siti Hajar Aflah Sharuddin, 40, with several more witnesses expected to testify. Deputy public prosecutors Christopher Enteri and Nadia Mohd Izhar prosecuted while lawyer Mohamad Zaiful Bahrin Kamde represented Siti Hajar Aflah. On Aug 21 last year, Siti Hajar Aflah pleaded not guilty to the charge.

items.title: DBKL: Jalan Yew bridge to close nightly from April 10–21 for reconstruction works
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 9 — The route along the Jalan Yew bridge will be temporarily closed to all vehicles from 10pm to 5am from April 10 to 21. The closure involves the stretch between TRX and Bulatan Pudu, as well as parts of Jalan Cochrane and Jalan Pasar (from the Masjid Alam Shah junction to the Petron station on Jalan Pasar). “During this period, road users are advised to use alternative routes via Jalan Pudu, Jalan Chan Sow Lin and Bulatan Kampung Pandan to reduce congestion and ensure smoother traffic flow.

items.title: Ulu Tiram police station attack: Cop found dead still clutching firearm – witness
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court was today told that one of the policemen killed in the Ulu Tiram police station attack two years ago was found dead still clutching his firearm. Ulu Tiram police station chief Inspector Mohd Hisyam Ezahar, 38, said that during the identification process, he saw Constable Muhamad Syafiq Ahmad Said, 24, lying dead with a head wound, still holding an HK MP5 firearm. The second prosecution witness said Syafiq's body was found near an armoured vehicle beside the station's enquiry counter.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues thunderstorm warning for seven states until 5pm today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning, including heavy rain and strong winds, for seven states until 5pm today. The affected states are Kedah, Perak, Kelantan, Terengganu, Pahang, Johor, and Sabah. In Kedah, the areas affected are Padang Terap, Sik, and Baling, while in Perak, the warning covers Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang, and Muallim.

items.title: Kelantan woman fined RM2,000 for storing pornographic videos on phone
items.summary: BACHOK, April 9 — A woman was fined RM2,000 by the Magistrates’ Court here today after pleading guilty to a charge of storing about 100 pornographic materials including pornographic videos in her cellphone last week. Earlier, deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Aiman Syahmi Yazid prosecuted while the accused was represented by National Legal Aid Foundation lawyer, Norizaidah Abdul Salam. The accused paid the fine set by the court.

items.title: Najib settles defamation suit with Dzulkefly over nepotism claim
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 9 — The 2022 defamation suit filed by Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad against former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak over a Facebook post alleging nepotism involving the health minister’s daughter’s appointment to a government-linked trust body was resolved in the High Court here today. Najib’s counsel, Tan Sri Muhammad Shafee Abdullah, informed Judge Mahazan Mat Taib that the matter had been resolved, with his client having prepared a statement following discussions with Dzulkefly. Najib then began reading his statement.

items.title: War in the Middle East: latest developments
items.summary: - China on Lebanon - A spokesman for the Chinese foreign ministry said "Lebanon's sovereignty and security should not be violated. - UK on Lebanon - British Foreign Minister Yvette Cooper said "we do strongly want to see the ceasefire extended to Lebanon." - France on Lebanon - France's foreign minister, Jean-Noel Barrot, condemned Israel's continuing strikes on Lebanon, calling them "all the more unacceptable as they undermine the temporary ceasefire reached yesterday between the United States and Iran."

items.title: Identical twin surgeons stay together despite being 'separated' once
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Like waves and the shore, this is an apt metaphor to describe the close bond between 40-year-old identical twins, Dr Mohd Azreen Ezainy Mohmad Sallih and Dr Mohd Azreen Ezairy. This time, the two surgeons are reunited at Bandar Dato' Onn Specialist Hospital, where Dr Azreen Ezainy joined his brother earlier this year. Dr Azreen Ezairy had already begun serving at the private hospital in 2023 as an otorhinolaryngology (ENT) surgeon, before his twin followed suit.

items.title: Deputy works minister: 30 federal projects in Borneo states expected to be completed in 2026 (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUCHING, April 9 — An estimated 30 development projects under the federal government in Sabah and Sarawak are expected to be completed within this year, said Datuk Seri Ahmad Maslan. Ahmad said the target follows the completion of 21 projects in 2025 worth RM466 million, of which 12 projects valued at RM203 million were in Sabah and nine projects worth RM263 million were in Sarawak. “Of these, 54 projects are located in Sarawak and 68 in Sabah,” he said.

items.title: Lack of inclusivity, clarity and enforcement undermined US-Iran ceasefire
items.summary: THE much-anticipated ceasefire between the United States and Iran — hailed as a fragile but necessary pause in a rapidly escalating conflict — appears to be unravelling almost as soon as it was announced. Israeli airstrikes on Lebanon — deadly in both scale and intensity — have effectively punctured any illusion that the ceasefire was comprehensive. Iran views the broader theatre — including Lebanon — as interconnected.

items.title: Iran, Saudi foreign ministers hold call on ties, regional issues
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has held a telephone conversation with his Saudi Arabian counterpart, Prince Faisal bin Farhan Al Saud, to discuss bilateral ties and regional developments, Teheran said on Thursday. Iran and Saudi Arabia have long been regional rivals, competing for political and economic influence for decades. The rivalry is also shaped by sectarian differences, with Sunni Islam dominant in Saudi Arabia and Shia Islam in Iran.

items.title: Single mother fined RM2k for storing obscene content on mobile phone
items.summary: BACHOK: A canteen assistant was fined RM2,000 by the magistrate's court here today after pleading guilty to storing about 100 obscene materials, including explicit videos, on her mobile phone on April 6. The accused, Siti Munirah Muhamad, 32, entered her plea after the charge was read before magistrate Fakhrul Razzi Abd Hamid. The prosecution was conducted by deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Aiman Syahmi Yazid, while the accused was represented by National Legal Aid Foundation (YBGK) lawyer Norizaidah Abdul Salam.

items.title: Kuching man fined RM3,000 for hurling petrol bomb at house in intimidation bid
items.summary: KUCHING, April 9 — A 23-year-old man was fined RM3,000 in default 10 months’ jail by the Magistrates’ Court here for threatening another man by hurling a petrol bomb at the latter’s house. Sulaiman and another man, who is still at large, committed the offence at a house in Kampung Demak Baru Phase 2, Jalan Bako at 5.35am on March 21. Upon investigation, Sulaiman was arrested around 1.50pm on April 1 at a house in Kampung Tupong Tengah, Petra Jaya.

items.title: Penampang eatery owner fined RM20,000 for stealing electricity
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 9 — An eatery operator was fined RM20,000 in default six months’ jail by the Sessions Court here for dishonestly using electricity last year by bypassing the cut-out fuse linked to the premises’ fuse box. Andrew Mali, 58, pleaded guilty before Judge Amir Shah Amir Hassan to a charge under Sub-Section 61(3)(c) of the Electricity Supply Enactment 2024, which is punishable under Sub-Section 61(3)(ii) of the same Enactment. The charge stated that Andrew committed the offence which caused Sabah Electricity Sdn Bhd (SESB) to charge him less for electricity or caused SESB to suffer a loss.

items.title: Trial resumes in June for teacher accused of causing alarm in Zara Qairina case
items.summary: SEPANG, April 9 — The Magistrates’ Court here today set June 11 to 12 as the date for the continuation of the trial of a school English teacher who was charged with causing public alarm by claiming that Zara Qairina Mahathir was put in a washing machine. Magistrate Suriani Ujang set the date for the first day of trial proceedings involving Siti Hajar Aflah Sharuddin, 40, with several more witnesses expected to appear to give their statements. The police officer also informed the court that he lodged a police report at the Kota Kinabalu International Airport Police Station, Sabah on August 6, 2025 after seeing two video recording posts via TikTok accounts related to the death of Zara Qairina which is currently under investigation.

items.title: Hajiji urges Sabah Bumiputera entrepreneurs to seize blue economy opportunities
items.summary: He said the blue economy will serve as a key driver under the Sabah Maju Jaya 2.0 (SMJ 2.0) roadmap, aimed at transforming the state’s economic structure towards high-value-added, inclusive and sustainable growth. Hajiji said the approach builds on Sabah’s maritime strengths and rich biodiversity, spanning strategic sectors including modern fisheries, aquaculture, marine biotechnology, maritime logistics and nature-based tourism. “During this period, Sabah recorded a historic milestone when state revenue nearly reached RM7 billion for the first time, with RM6.96 billion in 2022, RM6.97 billion in 2023, and remaining resilient at RM6.76 billion in 2025,” he said.

items.title: Suspended police corporal denies robbing woman at Kelantan grocery shop
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 9 — A police corporal suspended from duty pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here today to robbing a woman at a grocery shop in Kampung Kemumin last year Mohd Irfan Azieyadi Abdul Aziz, 45, entered the plea after the charge was read out to him before Judge Nik Habri Muhamad. He is accused of robbing Nik Zainab Nik Zul Azhar at Rohan Trading, Lot 1869, Jalan Sabak, Pengkalan Chepa, at about 10.30pm on November 7, 2025. Earlier, deputy public prosecutor Kamarul Hasyime Rosli proposed bail of RM15,000 with one surety, along with a condition requiring the accused to report monthly to the Kota Bharu District Police Headquarters (IPD).

items.title: Police tracking suspect over ‘Allah’ graffiti at temple in Melaka
items.summary: JASIN, April 9 — Police are tracking down a suspect believed to be involved in a case of graffiti involving the words ‘Allah’ and ‘Bismillahirrahmanirrahim’ at a Chinese temple in Pantai Siring here. Jasin district police chief Supt Lee Robert said initial information received from residents indicated that they had previously seen a man believed to be mentally unstable scribbling the words ‘Allah’ on walls and electricity poles. Checks on the land ownership status will also be carried out through the Jasin District and Land Office.

items.title: Caretaker pleads guilty to 15 abuse charges involving boys at unregistered children’s home in Batu Caves
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 9 — A caretaker at an unregistered children’s home pleaded guilty in two Sessions Courts in Ampang today to 15 counts of physical and non-physical sexual assault, as well as acts against the order of nature involving five boys. According to 12 of the charges, the man was accused of committing physical and non-physical sexual assault against five boys aged between eight and 13 at a welfare home run by an organisation in Batu Caves between November 2025 and March 27, 2026. For the offence of physical sexual assault, the charges were framed under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, which carries a maximum sentence of 20 years’ imprisonment and is also liable to whipping upon conviction.

items.title: High Court to decide on Shamsul Iskandar’s bid to transfer graft case on April 28
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 9 — The High Court today fixed April 28 to deliver its decision on the application by former senior political secretary to the Prime Minister, Datuk Seri Shamsul Iskandar Mohd Akin, to transfer the five corruption charges made against him from the Sessions Court to the High Court here. Judge Noor Ruwena Md Nurdin set the date after hearing submissions from Shamsul Iskandar’s counsel, Datuk Amer Hamzah Arshad, and deputy public prosecutor (DPP) Farah Ezlin Yusop Khan. “I would suggest April 28, 2026, (for decision),” she said.

items.title: Here’s why Malaysians will cut out cafes, eating out first, but not food deliveries in economic pinch
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 9 — When prices go up, will you stop ordering food deliveries first or stop eating outside at restaurants first? “When Malaysians have to choose between sacrificing the restaurant or the delivery app, they are consistently choosing to sacrifice the restaurant,” the research data arm of Rakuten Group said about its Malaysia Cost Pressure Pulse’s (MCPP) April 6 to April 8 survey results. The survey showed that those who would reduce spending on meals outside increased from 52.9 per cent in March to 61.6 per cent in April (which is the category with the largest jump with 8.7 percentage points), while the food delivery and quick commerce category only had a 1.5 percentage point increase (30 per cent in March will cut this out first, 32 per cent in April).

items.title: Temple says Aidilfitri open house is off, disputes Penang DCM’s replacement claim
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 9 — A Penang temple has flatly denied that any event will take place on April 11, pushing back against a state leader’s claim that a “unity” banquet would go ahead in place of its cancelled Aidilfitri open house. “There is no event organised by our party on April 11, 2026,” it said, adding that the planned “Unity Event” or Hari Raya Aidilfitri open house had been fully cancelled in respect of the Penang Islamic Religious Affairs Department (JHEAIPP). The denial comes hours after Penang Deputy Chief Minister I Datuk Mohamad Abdul Hamid said a “Majlis Jamuan Perpaduan” (Unity Banquet) would be held in Butterworth on April 11, organised by the Zhao Zi Long Cultural and Arts Association together with local residents.

items.title: HR Ministry: Private sector FWA must be implemented in accordance with Employment Act 1955
items.summary: The section, among others, gives employees the right to apply for flexible working arrangements and requires employers to provide written feedback within a specified period. In a statement today, Kesuma announced that FWA is not only limited to working from home (WFH) but also includes other forms of flexibility such as hybrid work models (combining work in the office and at home), flexible working hours and flexible work arrangements (compressed work weeks, shifts and output-based work). Kesuma said that 62 per cent of employers reported increased productivity during structured implementation of FWA while 70 per cent of employees recorded increased job satisfaction as a result of time and location flexibility.

items.title: Army officer fined RM1,700 over car park assault on female colleague in Melaka
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH, April 9 — An Army officer was fined RM1,700 by the Magistrates’ Court here today for causing hurt to a female colleague. Magistrate Dr Teoh Shu Yee meted out the fine, in default four months in jail, on R. Rajakogillan, 35, after he pleaded guilty to the charge. Rajakogillan was charged with voluntarily causing hurt to the 44-year-old woman by punching the back of the victim’s head three times at the Taman Terendak Heights car park, Masjid Tanah, at 7.30pm on October 5 last year.

items.title: DPM Zahid: New national disaster council marks shift from response to preparedness
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 9 — The establishment of the National Disaster Management Council (MPBN) reflects the government’s serious commitment to shifting from post-disaster reaction to prioritising preparedness, prevention and risk reduction, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said. Ahmad Zahid said the country needs a structure for faster strategic decisions, smoother coordination and seamless on-the-ground action — bridging any federal-state gaps. “Federal-state ties must tighten for closer, clearer collaboration, making the national disaster chain a unified team,” he added.

items.title: MACC remands former CEO, chairman over RM300m share deal scandal
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 9 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has remanded a former chief executive of a government statutory body and a company chairman over alleged collusion in a share sale deal linked to the agency. MACC’s Special Operations Division has also conducted raids at 13 locations across the Klang Valley, including government-linked agencies, companies of public interest, offices and residences of individuals identified in the probe. MACC Special Operations Division senior director Datuk Mohamad Zamri Zainul Abidin confirmed the arrests, adding that the case is being investigated under Section 16 of the MACC Act 2009.

items.title: World Bank: Malaysia’s BUDI95 fuel reform ‘step in the right direction’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 9 — Malaysia’s targeted fuel subsidy reform under BUDI Madani RON95 (BUDI95) is a step in the right direction amid the West Asia crisis as the government seeks to better manage fiscal pressures while ensuring subsidies are distributed more efficiently. World Bank lead economist for Malaysia Apurva Sanghi said adjusting fuel quota limits represents a more targeted approach, helping ensure subsidies are more fairly allocated across different income groups. “Fuel subsidies in Malaysia are regressive, with the T10, T20 benefitting much more compared to others.

items.title: Serkam ‘shrine’ with ‘Allah’ wording to be demolished today, says Melaka exco
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 9 — A structure, thought to be a shrine, in Serkam, Jasin, will be demolished this afternoon by its owner, Melaka authorities said. According to Berita Harian , State Education, Higher Education and Religious Affairs exco Datuk Rahmad Mariman said the demolition, scheduled for about 2.30pm, will be led by Serkam assemblyman Datuk Zaidi Attan and monitored by local authorities. The matter came to light after a group of anglers noticed the word “Allah” at the shrine along Jalan Jeti Anjung Batu–Pantai Siring last night and alerted the authorities.

items.title: World Bank: Malaysia doesn’t need stimulus despite Middle East crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 9 — Malaysia does not need stimulus amid the West Asia crisis, said the World Bank’s lead economist for Malaysia, Apurva Sanghi. He said providing a stimulus now would only add to the inflationary impulse, noting that inflation in Malaysia remains stable and contained at present. Apurva said Malaysia’s inflation remains stable and well contained, registering 1.6 per cent in January 2026.
